name: Traditional Flan Custard
method: Preheat the oven to 325 degrees F.
To prepare caramel sauce, pour sugar into a pan over the medium
heat. Stir constantly.
When it is brown and fully melted, it will become caramel.
Once the color reaches golden brown, transfer it to the pie plate.
To prepare the custard, whisk the eggs for one minute or until they are
frothy.
Gradually put condensed milk in it, then the evaporated milk. Slowly
mix the vanilla and sugar.
To bake the flan, put the custard mixture in the caramel pie plate.
Put the pie plate in a baking dish.
Bake the flan for around 45 minutes. Use a knife to check its status. If
it is clean, then your flan is ready.
Remove the flan from the baking dish. Let it cool for
overnight in the refrigerator for better taste.
Serve and enjoy!
8 Mexican Churros
Instructions
1 cup of water
4 cups of vegetable oil
1/2 cups of salted butter
1/4 tsp. of salt
1 tsp. of vanilla extract
1 cup flour
1 cup of sugar
3 eggs
1 tsp. of ground cinnamon
Instructions
Make the dough by mixing water, vanilla, salt, and butter in a
saucepan. When the mixture starts to simmer, turn off the heat.
Add eggs one by one, mashing and stirring the dough until it is fully
incorporated.
Put the mixture to a pastry bag.
Put the dough into the hot oil.
Cook it until it is puffy. Cook evenly from all sides. It will take 2-4
minutes.
Carefully take out churro from the oil, and use a paper towel to drain
When churros are cooled, put the cinnamon and sugar into a plastic
Put churros in it and shake until they are coated.
